example: Teamwork is an integral part of success in any organization.
mnemonic: integr(온전한, 라틴어 integer) + al = 온전한 → 필수적인. integr(온전한): integrate, integrity, integer(정수=온전한 수)

example: The committee tried to reconcile the disparate views of its members.
mnemonic: dis(분리) + par(동등한) + ate = 동등하지 않은 → 이질적인. par(동등): compare, pair, parity, disparity

example: The company's antediluvian policies were desperately in need of modernization.
mnemonic: ante(이전) + diluv(홍수, 라틴어 diluvium) + ian = 대홍수 이전의 → 아주 오래된. diluv(홍수): deluge(대홍수). ante(이전): antecedent

example: In ancient rituals, animals were immolated to appease the gods.
mnemonic: im(위에) + mol(곡물, 라틴어 mola=갈린 곡물) + ate = 제물 위에 곡물을 뿌리다 → 제물로 바치다

example: The dolorous melody brought tears to the eyes of the audience.
mnemonic: dolor(고통, 라틴어) + ous = 고통스러운 → 슬픈. dolor(고통): doleful, condolence, indolent

example: The canny investor always managed to buy low and sell high.
mnemonic: can(할 수 있다, 알다) + ny = 잘 아는 → 영리한. know와 같은 어원 계열

example: Alchemists tried to transmute base metals into gold.
mnemonic: trans(넘어서) + mute(변하다, 라틴어 mutare) = 넘어서 변하다 → 변환하다. mut(변하다): mutate, immutable, commute

example: The government launched a campaign to extirpate corruption from public offices.
mnemonic: ex(밖으로) + stirp(뿌리, 라틴어 stirps) + ate = 뿌리를 밖으로 뽑다 → 근절하다

example: The defunct company's offices have been converted into apartments.
mnemonic: de(완전히) + funct(기능하다, 라틴어 fungi) = 기능을 다한 → 소멸한. funct(기능): function, malfunction, dysfunctional

example: The offer comes with an important caveat: the warranty is limited to one year.
mnemonic: 라틴어 caveat(조심하라, cavere=조심하다의 접속법). 조심하라는 경고 → 단서. caution도 같은 어원

example: She approached the interview with considerable trepidation.
mnemonic: trepid(떨리는, 라틴어 trepidus) + ation = 떨림 → 공포. intrepid(대담한) = in(아닌)+trepid(떨리는)

example: The redoubtable champion had not lost a single match in five years.
mnemonic: re(다시) + doubt(두려워하다, 라틴어 dubitare) + able = 다시 두려움을 느끼게 하는 → 강력한. doubt(두려움, 의심): dubious, indubitable

example: The surgeon excised the damaged tissue during the operation.
mnemonic: ex(밖으로) + cise(자르다, 라틴어 caedere) = 밖으로 잘라내다 → 절제하다. cis/cid(자르다): incise, decide, concise

example: The victor showed magnanimity by pardoning the defeated rebels.
mnemonic: magn(큰, 라틴어 magnus) + anim(정신) + ity = 큰 정신 → 관대함. magnanimous(관대한)

example: The nascent democracy faced many challenges in its early years.
mnemonic: nasc(태어나다, 라틴어 nasci) + ent = 막 태어난 → 초기의. nasc/nat(태어나다): native, nation, natal, innate, renaissance

example: As a neophyte in the field, she had much to learn from her mentors.
mnemonic: neo(새로운, 그리스어) + phyte(식물, 그리스어 phyton) = 새로 심긴 → 초심자. neo(새로운): neonatal. phyt(식물): epiphyte

example: The community's insular attitude prevented them from accepting new ideas.
mnemonic: '인슐러' → 'insul(섬=island)+ar' 섬처럼 고립된 편협한!

example: The idea of building a bridge to the moon is absolutely ludicrous.
mnemonic: lud(놀다, 라틴어 ludere) + icrous = 놀이처럼 우스꽝스러운. lud(놀다): elude, illusion, prelude

example: There is a deep antipathy between the two rival groups.
mnemonic: anti(반대) + pathy(감정) = 반대 감정 → 반감. path(감정): sympathy, empathy, apathy

example: Excessive regulation can be inimical to economic growth.
mnemonic: '이니미컬' → 'in+imic(적=enemy)+al' 적처럼 적대적인!

example: The performance earned plaudits from critics and audiences alike.
mnemonic: 라틴어 plaudite(박수치라), plaudere(박수치다). 박수 → 갈채, 칭찬. plaud(박수): applaud, plausible

example: The contumacious defendant was held in contempt of court.
mnemonic: con(완전히) + tumac(부풀다, 라틴어 tumere) + ious = 완전히 부풀어 오른 듯 반항하는 → 완강한. tum(부풀다): tumult, tumor

example: His discursive writing style made it hard to follow his main argument.
mnemonic: dis(떨어져) + curs(달리다, 라틴어 currere) + ive = 여기저기 달리는 → 산만한. curr/curs(달리다): discourse, cursor, excursion

example: The speaker made a felicitous choice of words that delighted the audience.
mnemonic: felic(행복한, 라틴어 felix) + itous = 행복한 → 적절한. felic(행복): felicity, infelicitous

example: A multitude of fans gathered outside the stadium for the concert.
mnemonic: multi(많은) + tude = 많음 → 다수. multi(많은): multiply, multimedia, multifaceted

example: The judge delivered the verdict in a magisterial tone.
mnemonic: magister(스승, 라틴어) + ial = 스승다운 → 위엄 있는. magist(큰, 주인): master, magistrate

example: Bureaucratic inertia prevented the government from implementing reforms quickly.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + ert(기술, 라틴어 ars) + ia = 기술이 없는 상태 → 무기력, 관성. art(기술): art, inert, expert

example: The company was fined for adulterating its products with cheap ingredients.
mnemonic: ad(~쪽으로) + ulter(다른, 라틴어 alter) + ate = 다른 것을 섞다 → 변질시키다. alter 어근: alternate, alteration

example: The refractory patient refused to follow the doctor's instructions.
mnemonic: re(뒤로) + fract(부수다, 라틴어 frangere) + ory = 뒤로 부수며 저항하는 → 반항적인. fract(부수다): fracture, fraction

example: The diffident student rarely spoke up in class discussions.
mnemonic: dif(부정) + fid(믿다, 신뢰) + ent = 자신을 믿지 못하는 → 소심한. fid(믿다): confident, fidelity, confide

example: Her indomitable spirit helped her overcome every obstacle in her path.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + domit(길들이다, 라틴어 domare) + able = 길들일 수 없는 → 불굴의. dom(길들이다): dominate, domestic

example: The evidence against the suspect was incontrovertible.
mnemonic: '인컨트로버터블' → 'in(아닌)+controvert(반박)+ible' 반박할 수 없는 명백한!

example: She is an expert in constitutional jurisprudence.
mnemonic: juris(법, 라틴어 jus) + prudence(분별) = 법에 대한 분별 → 법학. jur(법): jury, justice, judge, jurisdiction

example: The discovery of penicillin was a case of scientific serendipity.
mnemonic: 호레이스 월폴이 동화 'Serendip의 세 왕자'에서 만든 말. 우연히 행운을 발견하다 → 뜻밖의 발견

example: The editorial excoriated the government for its handling of the crisis.
mnemonic: ex(밖으로) + cori(가죽, 라틴어 corium) + ate = 가죽을 벗기다 → 가혹하게 비판하다

example: The connection between the two events is tenuous at best.
mnemonic: tenu(얇은, 라틴어 tenuis) + ous = 얇은 → 희박한. tenu(얇은): attenuate, thin

example: She finagled her way into the exclusive event without an invitation.
mnemonic: 영국 방언 fainaigue(속이다)에서 유래. 교묘하게 속여서 → 얻어내다

example: The poet wrote an elegy for his deceased friend.
mnemonic: 그리스어 elegeia(비가), elegos(슬픈 노래). 슬픈 노래 → 비가, 애도시. elegiac(비가의)

example: She sublimated her grief into creative artistic expression.
mnemonic: sub(아래에서) + lim(문턱, 한계) + ate = 한계를 넘어 높이다 → 승화시키다. sublime(숭고한)과 같은 어근

example: The teacher showed great forbearance with the disruptive students.
mnemonic: '포베어런스' → 'for+bear(견디다)+ance' 견뎌내는 인내!

example: The office toady always agreed with everything the boss said.
mnemonic: toad(두꺼비) + y. 옛날 돌팔이 의사의 조수가 두꺼비 독을 먹는 시늉 → 비굴하게 아첨하는 사람

example: The conductor led the orchestra with great panache and flair.
mnemonic: 이탈리아어 pennacchio(깃털 장식). 모자의 화려한 깃털 → 당당함, 화려한 솜씨

example: The grandeur of the ancient cathedral left all visitors speechless.
mnemonic: grand(큰, 웅장한) + eur = 웅장함. grand(큰): aggrandize, grandiose

example: The wine was praised by cognoscenti for its extraordinary complexity and depth.
mnemonic: cognosc(알다, 라틴어 cognoscere) + enti = 아는 사람들 → 감식가들. cogn(알다): cognition, recognize, cognizant

example: The journalist plumbed the depths of the corruption scandal.
mnemonic: 라틴어 plumbum(납). 납추로 깊이를 재다 → 철저히 조사하다. plumb(납): plumber, plummet, aplomb

example: She is considered a paragon of virtue in her community.
mnemonic: para(옆에) + gon(가다) = 옆에서 비교해도 뛰어난 → 모범, 귀감

example: The negotiations reached an impasse when neither side would compromise.
mnemonic: '임패스' → 'im(아닌)+pass(지나가다)' 지나갈 수 없는 교착 상태!

example: The critics extolled the young artist's innovative techniques.
mnemonic: '익스톨' → 'ex(높이)+toll(들다)' 높이 들어올려 극찬!

example: He tends to aggrandize his achievements in conversations.
mnemonic: ag(~쪽으로) + grand(큰) + ize = 크게 만들다 → 확대하다, 과장하다

example: He felt no compunction about lying to protect his friend.
mnemonic: com(완전히) + punct(찌르다, 라틴어 pungere) + ion = 양심이 찔리는 것 → 가책. punct(찌르다): puncture, pungent, point
